channel.title: Rest-FMRI on Feng Sang | 桑峰
channel.description: Recent content in Rest-FMRI on Feng Sang | 桑峰

item.title: 文献笔记-大脑功能网络中体现的左右半球和性别差异
item.description: Title Hemisphere- and gender-related differences in small-world brain networks: A resting-state functional MRI study Results Male have a large normalized clustering coefficient in the right hemispheric network, but a lower clustering coefficient in the left hemispheric network. Significant hemisphere-related differences in the regional nodal characteristics: frontal and occipital regions (leftward asymmetry), temporal regions (rightward asymmetry). Conclustions Topological organization of human brain functional networks is associated with gender and hemispheres. Notes Alterations of brain asymmetries are associated with behavior changes in normal aging and in various neuropsychiatric and neurological diseases such as schizophrenia, stroke and dyslexia.
